summary: The serum glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase level increases significantly during a heart attack due to cellular damage from ischemia-reperfusion injury affecting cardiac muscle cells directly. This enzyme also rises when there are liver injuries that might be secondary effects of severe coronary artery disease leading to reduced blood flow through vital organs like the liver. To pinpoint exactly which condition causes this elevation would require further diagnostic testing beyond just knowing about an increase alone since both conditions can elevate it but for different This enzyme also rises when there are liver injuries that might be secondary effects of severe coronary artery disease leading to reduced blood flow through vital organs like the liver.
